Title: Host metabolites explain microbiome variation between different rice genotypes

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates trans-cinnamate and cinnamoyl-CoA to form 4-hydroxycinnamic acid and p-coumaroyl-CoA, supplying precursors for ferulic acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Shapes rice phyllosphere microbiome; inhibits Burkholderiales and favors Pseudomonadales enrichment.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Natural haplotypes of OsC4H2 were associated with leaf ferulic acid concentration differences across 110 rice varieties. 2. Haplotypes linked to higher ferulic acid showed lower relative abundance of Burkholderiales. 3. CRISPR knockout of OsC4H2 decreased ferulic acid, while overexpression increased ferulic acid and 4-hydroxycinnamic acid. 4. OsC4H2 negatively regulated Burkholderiales and overexpression increased Pseudomonadales abundance, demonstrating microbiome-shaping effects via phenylpropanoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ferulic acid accumulation and microbiome shifts toward lower Burkholderiales and higher Pseudomonadales
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth under controlled conditions; in vitro ferulic acid treatment of bacterial isolates (0-1 mM) supported metabolite-mediated effects.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful microbiome gene for breeding or engineering rice genotypes with higher ferulic acid to suppress Burkholderiales and enrich beneficial Pseudomonadales; haplotype selection or overexpression/editing could be applied.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Four major haplotypes were identified; six promoter-region nucleotide variations and one coding-region SNP distinguished major haplotypes. c4h2 knockout did not significantly reduce 4-hydroxycinnamic acid relative to WT, but overexpression increased it.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SNP-metabolite association analysis; haplotype analysis; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; overexpression; metabolite profiling; 16S rRNA microbiome profiling; correlation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing, PRJCA025536; SNP dataset from C-RDP-II (published previously); targeted metabolomics; scripts at Zenodo 10115039 and 11072931

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step of general phenylpropanoid/lignin precursor pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to form trans-cinnamate, the entry precursor for downstream 4-hydroxycinnamic acid and ferulic acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Shapes rice phyllosphere microbiome; inhibits Burkholderiales and favors Pseudomonadales enrichment.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Natural haplotypes of OsPAL06 were associated with significant differences in leaf ferulic acid concentration across 110 rice varieties. 2. The high-ferulic-acid haplotype showed lower Burkholderiales abundance. 3. CRISPR knockout of OsPAL06 reduced ferulic acid and 4-hydroxycinnamic acid, while overexpression increased both metabolites. 4. OsPAL06 negatively regulated Burkholderiales and positively regulated Pseudomonadales in the rice phyllosphere.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ferulic acid and 4-hydroxycinnamic acid biosynthesis; suppresses Burkholderiales and promotes Pseudomonadales in the rice phyllosphere.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth under controlled conditions; in vitro ferulic acid treatment of bacterial isolates (0-1 mM) supported metabolite-mediated effects.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful microbiome gene for breeding or engineering rice genotypes with higher ferulic acid to suppress Burkholderiales and enrich beneficial Pseudomonadales; favorable haplotypes, editing, or overexpression are applicable.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Three major haplotypes were identified; eight promoter-region nucleotide variations and one coding-region SNP distinguished the high-ferulic-acid haplotype. pal06 knockout significantly reduced both ferulic acid and 4-hydroxycinnamic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key entry/committed step supported by enzyme assignment; rate-limiting not directly established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SNP-metabolite association analysis; haplotype analysis; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; overexpression; metabolite profiling; 16S rRNA microbiome profiling; correlation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing, PRJCA025536; SNP dataset from C-RDP-II (published previously); targeted metabolomics; scripts at Zenodo 10115039 and 11072931
